headline: Thrilling Update: We're Still Doing Nothing.
subtext: Pretty sure we've just spent three hours scheduling another meeting.
body:
**Headline: Thrilling Update: We're Still Doing Nothing.**

Little Puddleton Council has reaffirmed its unwavering commitment to its strategic "Maintain Status Quo" initiative, delivering another year of uninterrupted non-action. A recent press release lauded the "consistent performance in avoiding tangible outcomes," a sentiment echoed by the Sub-Committee for Proactive Inactivity, already celebrating its seventh year of deliberations.

Council spokesperson, Brenda Plinth, stated, "Our residents value predictability. Why fix what isn't actively collapsing when you can instead observe its dignified decay?" Specific issues, like the glow-in-the-dark pond or Mr. Henderson’s interpretive dance property disputes, are "elegantly preserved for future contemplation."

Residents, largely indifferent, expressed quiet satisfaction. "It's nice to know where you stand," commented local baker, Geoff Crumble, "which is exactly where you were last year." The council is exploring a "Zero-Impact Zone" for the borough, where proposals for change automatically enter an "indefinite pre-consultation holding pattern."

Critics, dubbed "action agitators," warn of civic stagnation. Yet, analysts suggest Little Puddleton pioneers governance by omission. Future plans include a "Non-Milestone" ceremony and a potential Department of Utter Futility, ensuring the thrilling update remains perennially the same.
